findings.evidence: The endpoint /invoice accepts invoice_id and current_user_id as query parameters but does not verify that the current_user_id matches the owner_id of the requested invoice. This allows any user to access any invoice by changing the invoice_id parameter.
findings.recommendation: Implement authorization checks to ensure that the current_user_id matches the owner_id of the invoice before returning the invoice data.

summary: The code lacks proper access control, allowing users to access invoices they do not own by manipulating the invoice_id parameter, resulting in an Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR) vulnerability.
